test.command: cd /home/realnigga/ORCHESTRATOR-FRONTEND-MISSION/orchestrator-frontend && npx vitest run src/pages/auth src/pages/ProfilePage src/layouts src/lib/__tests__/portal-routing.test.ts
test.note: Full suite (bun run test --run) times out due to known vitest infrastructure issue documented in AGENTS.md. Auth-specific tests (189 tests, 7 files) all pass in 1.84s.

nonBlockingIssues.description: FirstPasswordChangePage.tsx and ResetPasswordPage.tsx use Tailwind hardcoded red colors (border-red-300, text-red-600) for password validation errors instead of CSS variables. LoginPage uses CSS variables (var(--color-warning-bg)) for its error states, creating inconsistency.
ProfilePage password section uses Camera icon instead of semantically correct Lock or KeyRound icon (pre-existing issue).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on error-state color conventions: when to use Tailwind semantic color utilities (text-red-600, border-red-300) vs CSS variables (var(--color-error-*)). The current convention only says 'NEVER hardcode hex colors' but Tailwind utilities are technically not hex — clarify whether Tailwind reds are acceptable for validation errors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: FirstPasswordChangePage uses border-red-300/text-red-600 for password mismatch errors, while LoginPage uses var(--color-warning-bg) for lockout banner. Inconsistency suggests lack of clear convention for error state colors.

rejectedObservations.observation: Auth page label strings duplicated between components and test files without centralization (no i18n keys).
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected — This is a code style preference, not a systemic issue. The current pattern of inline strings with test assertions is standard React testing practice. Adding i18n centralization would be a separate initiative outside the scope of this mission.

rejectedObservations.observation: frontend-worker skill should clarify handling of pre-implemented features.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected — This is a skill improvement suggestion, not a codebase or convention issue. The orchestrator can decide whether to update skills based on the worker's own feedback.
